title: Velvety Dutch Rice Pilaf
description: A creamy and savory Dutch pilaf dish featuring rice, jocheong (Korean soy paste), and rice syrup, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This recipe is free from animal products and ideal for Jain dietary needs.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger; sauté for another minute.
Add the long-grain rice and stir to coat with oil and mix with the onion-garlic-ginger mixture.
Add the vegetable broth, salt, and black pepper to the saucepan.
Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes.
While the rice cooks, whisk together the jocheong and rice syrup in a small bowl until smooth.
After 15 minutes, stir the jocheong-rice syrup mixture into the cooked rice.
Continue to simmer, uncovered, for an additional 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the toasted sesame oil and adjust seasoning as needed.
Serve the velvety Dutch rice pilaf hot, garnished with chopped green onions or toasted sesame seeds, if desired.
